newsGap.title: News Gap Analyzer
newsGap.subtitle: Discover supply chain stories the market is missing
newsGap.description: Our AI cross-references supply chain data with media coverage to find material events that haven't received adequate attention.
newsGap.heroExplanation: When institutional investors are quietly accumulating a stock that the media is ignoring, that divergence often precedes significant price movement. We measure this gap.
newsGap.ibVsMediaDesc: Companies in the <accent>top-left quadrant</accent> (high IB activity, low media) are the strongest signals.
newsGap.theTheoryText: Institutional investors file 13F reports quarterly, disclosing their holdings. These filings reveal what the most sophisticated investors are buying and selling. Meanwhile, media coverage reflects what the general public and retail investors are paying attention to.
newsGap.whySilenceMattersText: When there is a large gap between institutional buying activity and media attention, it often means smart money has identified an opportunity that the broader market has not yet recognized. The silence is not random - it is a signal that the market has mispriced something.
newsGap.noNewsIn30Days: No coverage in last 30 days — strong silence signal
newsGap.staticDataTooltip: Alpha Vantage news and EDGAR 13F data not yet collected. Displaying research-based static data. Updates on next cron run (02:00 UTC).
newsGap.dataScheduleDetail: Institutional holdings (EDGAR 13F) — Daily 02:00 UTC cron · Media coverage (Alpha Vantage) — 25 tickers/day · Static baseline: 2026-Q1 research

about.title: About Flowvium
about.missionText: We believe supply chain transparency shouldn't be a luxury. Flowvium democratizes access to supply chain intelligence, empowering investors, analysts, and businesses to make informed decisions.
about.storyText: Born from the realization that global supply chains are the hidden backbone of the economy, Flowvium was built to make these invisible networks visible. We combine alternative data, AI analysis, and intuitive visualization to reveal the connections that matter.
about.teamText: We're a team of engineers, data scientists, and supply chain experts passionate about bringing transparency to global trade.
about.valuesTransparencyText: We believe information asymmetry creates unfair markets. Our goal is to level the playing field.
about.valuesAccuracyText: Every data point is verified and cross-referenced. We'd rather show less than show wrong.
about.valuesAccessibilityText: Powerful tools should be available to everyone, not just institutions with deep pockets.
about.contactText: Have questions or want to partner with us? We'd love to hear from you.

intelligence.askPlaceholder: e.g. Why is Wall Street so profitable? Who benefits from QE?
intelligence.cotSubtitle: Non-commercial (speculator) net = Long − Short. Positive → bullish bias.
intelligence.cotLegend: Non-commercial net = speculator (hedge funds, CTAs) longs minus shorts. CFTC publishes weekly on Fridays for positions as of the prior Tuesday.

osint.pageSubtitle: Key figures · Blockchain wallets · International sanctions · Corporate networks — live monitoring
osint.legalWarning: For educational, research, and lawful investigative purposes only
osint.socialDesc: Key figures' latest statements & X posts — 30min cache
osint.noNews: No related news at this time
osint.loadingSanctions: Loading OFAC sanctions list (first load may take a moment)...
osint.guideIntro: OSINT (Open Source Intelligence) is a methodology for collecting and analyzing publicly available information. It is used in financial crime investigations, corporate due diligence, journalism, and compliance.
osint.guideMethod1Desc: Automatically collects recent statements from market-influencing figures (Trump, Powell, Musk, etc.) from news feeds. AI automatically classifies tone (hawkish/dovish/bullish/bearish).
osint.guideMethod2Desc: Automatically displays real-time balances and transactions for notable wallets (Satoshi, Binance, Vitalik, etc.). You can also enter any wallet address for direct analysis.
osint.guideMethod3Desc: Updated daily with the full U.S. Treasury OFAC SDN (Specially Designated Nationals) list. Auto-categorized by program: Russia, Iran, North Korea, China, Terrorism.
osint.guideMethod4Desc: Automatically displays global corporate registrations for notable entities (Gazprom, Huawei, etc.). Useful for identifying offshore registrations and shell company structures.
osint.guideMethod5Desc: Owners of real estate, yachts, and private jets can be identified through public registry and AIS/flight tracking data.
osint.guideMethod6Desc: Track legal and financial relationships via SEC EDGAR (U.S. filings), PACER (federal court records), and corporate registries worldwide.

report.title: AI Report
report.subtitle: Global money flows · Institutional signals · Company analysis
report.tipFg: Fear & Greed Index (0-100). ≤25=extreme fear, ≥75=extreme greed. Contrarian signal for market sentiment.
report.tipSpy: S&P 500 ETF 1-week return. Short-term direction of the US large-cap market.
report.tipCurve: 10Y minus 2Y Treasury yield spread (bp). Negative (inverted) historically signals recession. Positive=normal.
report.tipHyOas: High-yield bond risk premium. >5%=credit stress, 4-5%=caution, <4%=safe. Higher = more credit risk.
report.tipFomc: CME FedWatch probability of a rate cut at the next FOMC meeting. Market's bet on Fed policy.
report.tipCycle: Business cycle based on GDP growth & CPI. Goldilocks=ideal, Stagflation=low growth+high inflation, Overheating=hot, Recession=contraction.
report.pageDesc: AI-driven buy strategy and portfolio combining all market indicators
report.disclaimer: This report is AI-generated reference material based on public data. Investment decisions are solely your responsibility; returns are not guaranteed.
report.error: Failed to load report — please try again later

insider.title: Real-time Flow · Insider Activity
insider.subtitle: SEC Form 4 + 13D/13G + options flow + Korean institutional — Bloomberg-grade real-time capital tracking
insider.explainerTitle: Bloomberg can't give real-time 13F either — 45-day legal lag
insider.explainerBody: But combining Form 4 (D+2) insider trades, Schedule 13D/13G (5%+ crossings), options unusual flow, and KRX foreign/institutional net-buys, you get live institutional signals. This page stitches them together.
insider.optionsLockedBody: This requires the Unusual Whales API ($48/mo). It unlocks once the $200/mo donation goal in the footer is reached — until then, insider trades, 13D/G alerts, and Korea flow run entirely on free SEC/KRX data.
insider.sources: Sources: SEC EDGAR (Form 4, 13D/13G), Unusual Whales (options), KRX Data System · 15-30min cache
insider.nportExplainer: Form N-PORT is the monthly holdings disclosure for mutual funds — 3× faster than 13F (quarterly) with a 60-day lag, so you see what Fidelity / Vanguard / BlackRock bought last month. Aggregated by tracked ticker.
insider.blocksLockedBody: Polygon.io Starter $29/mo (15-min delayed) or Advanced $199/mo (realtime). Unlocks once the $200/mo donation goal in the footer is reached. Even on the free tier the other 6 tabs are live.
insider.korPeriodDesc: Cumulative foreign/inst net buy over {days} trading days
insider.koreaFallback: Price change data only (foreign/inst net buy unavailable)

macro.fwDesc: Market-implied probability of rate decisions at each meeting
macro.fwFootnote: 💡 Probabilities derived from Fed Funds futures — CME FedWatch methodology
macro.mrRiskOnDesc: Healthy credit · optimistic consumers · normalized yield curve → risk assets favored
macro.mrNeutralDesc: Credit stable but mixed sentiment → direction unclear
macro.mrRiskOffDesc: Credit stress · weak consumption · inverted yield curve → safe-haven assets favored
macro.spFootnote: Source: Yahoo Finance v8 · SPDR Sector ETFs · 4h cache
macro.miTitle: Key Economic Indicators Moving Rates & Markets
macro.miDesc: Shows how each data release creates cascading effects on stocks, bonds, the dollar, and gold.
macro.miLaymanHint: 💡 Tap the button for a plain-English explanation.
macro.miNoCascade: In-line result — market already priced this in, no major reaction expected.
macro.lCpiWhat: Shows how much prices of everyday goods — groceries, restaurants, etc. — have risen compared to a year ago.
macro.lCpiWhy: The Fed's key gauge for deciding whether to raise or cut interest rates.
macro.lCpiGood: Below forecast → price stability → rate cut hopes → stocks can rally
macro.lCpiBad: Above forecast → overheating inflation → rate hikes → stocks & bonds fall together
macro.lPceWhat: Similar to CPI but the Fed's preferred inflation measure. Captures how much people are actually spending more accurately.
macro.lPceWhy: The Fed Chair has publicly called it "our preferred inflation gauge."
macro.lPceGood: Below forecast → rate cuts may come sooner
macro.lPceBad: Above forecast → rate cuts delayed → growth stocks under pressure
macro.lNfpWhat: Counts how many new jobs the U.S. economy added last month (excluding farming).
macro.lNfpWhy: Jobs are the economy's thermometer. Strong gains = healthy economy. The Fed watches this number closely.
macro.lNfpGood: Below forecast → slowdown → Fed cut pressure → bonds rally
macro.lNfpBad: Above forecast → overheating → Fed cuts harder to justify → growth stocks short-term pressure
macro.lFomcWhat: The Fed's meeting where they decide to raise, cut, or hold interest rates. Held 8 times a year.
macro.lFomcWhy: The single most market-moving event — directly impacts every asset class in the world.
macro.lFomcGood: More dovish than expected (cut/hold) → stocks, crypto, gold rally together
macro.lFomcBad: More hawkish than expected (hike/tough) → stocks plunge, dollar surges, EM capital flight
macro.lGdpWhat: Measures how much the entire U.S. economy grew in a quarter, expressed as an annualized rate.
macro.lGdpWhy: The economy's report card. Above 2% = healthy. Two negative quarters in a row = official recession.
macro.lGdpGood: Above forecast → stronger earnings expectations → positive for stocks
macro.lGdpBad: Below forecast → recession fears → buy safe havens (gold, bonds)
macro.lIsmWhat: Surveys purchasing managers at manufacturers: "Is business getting better?" Above 50 = expansion, below = contraction.
macro.lIsmWhy: Famous leading indicator that tends to move 2–3 months ahead of the actual economy.
macro.lIsmGood: Above 50 + beats expectations → manufacturing recovery → industrials & commodities rise
macro.lIsmBad: Below 50 + misses → manufacturing contraction → cyclical stocks fall
macro.lRetailWhat: Tracks how much U.S. consumers spent shopping last month. Consumer spending = ~70% of U.S. GDP.
macro.lRetailWhy: Consumer spending directly feeds into corporate revenues → earnings → stock prices.
macro.lRetailGood: Above forecast → strong consumption → retail & consumer stocks rally
macro.lRetailBad: Below forecast → consumption slowing → recession concerns
macro.lPpiWhat: Measures how much raw material and parts costs for manufacturers have risen. Comes out 1–2 months before CPI.
macro.lPpiWhy: PPI rises → companies raise prices → CPI rises later. Used as a CPI leading indicator.
macro.lPpiGood: Below forecast → cost pressures easing → better corporate margins expected
macro.lPpiBad: Above forecast → signals future CPI spike → tightening fears
macro.lUnrateWhat: The percentage of workers who want a job but can't find one.
macro.lUnrateWhy: "Maximum employment" is one of the Fed's two mandates. Very low unemployment → wage inflation risk.
macro.lUnrateGood: Rising → economic slowdown → Fed cut pressure
macro.lUnrateBad: Too low → wage pressure → inflation → rate hike risk
macro.lIgWhat: The gap between investment-grade corporate bond yields and U.S. Treasury yields — the 'risk premium' companies pay to borrow.
macro.lIgWhy: Wider spread = higher credit stress. Above 1.5% = alert, above 2% = crisis territory.
macro.lIgGood: Spread narrowing → cheaper corporate borrowing → investment & hiring expand
macro.lIgBad: Spread widening → higher borrowing costs → investment contracts → leads stocks lower
macro.lHyWhat: The gap between high-yield (junk bond) yields and U.S. Treasuries — the "fear gauge" for the riskiest borrowers.
macro.lHyWhy: Above 5% = recession warning. Historically peaked above 10% during major crises.
macro.lHyGood: Spread narrowing → risk appetite returning → stocks & high-yield bonds rally together
macro.lHyBad: Above 5% → credit market panic → corporate defaults rise → stocks plunge
macro.volSource: Source: CBOE · Yahoo Finance · 30min cache · VXST=9d / VIX=30d / VXMT=6m
